title: Garlicky Argentinian Peanuts Empanadas
description: Romantic empanadas from Argentina with a crunchy peanut crust and rich garlicky filling. Serve as a snack or light meal.

ingredients.name: flour
ingredients.notes: make sure to use gluten-free flour for a fodmap-friendly diet

instructions: Combine peanut oil, garlic, and onion in a pan over medium heat. Cook for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken breast to the pan and cook until it is fully cooked through, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the peanuts to the pan and stir to combine with the chicken and onion mixture.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, egg, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Roll out the dough on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Spoon a small amount of the chicken and peanut mixture onto one half of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling, pressing the edges together with a fork to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and sprinkle with salt.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 20-25 minutes, or until the empanadas are golden brown and crispy on the bottom.
Let the empanadas cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the empanadas warm, garnished with a sprinkle of paprika and a side of salsa if desired.
You can also freeze the empanadas for up to 2 months and bake them straight from the freezer.
To freeze, place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and put them in the freezer until they are frozen solid, then transfer them to a freezer-safe bag or container.
To bake frozen empanadas, add 5-10 minutes to the baking time and keep an eye on them to make sure they don't burn.
